title: Java Applet API Removal
description: Recognize the Java 26 removal of the obsolete Applet API.
learningGoals: Recognize that java.applet.Applet is removed rather than merely deprecated in Java 26.
Use reflection-style probes in migration checks without importing removed applet types.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens to source code that imports java.applet.Applet on Java 26?
choices: It compiles with a deprecation warning only
It fails because the Applet API has been removed
completionChecks.explanation: JEP 504 removes the Applet API. Code that imports or extends java.applet.Applet must be removed or migrated.

completionChecks.prompt: Why does this lesson use Class.forName("java.applet.Applet") instead of an import?
choices: The string is faster than a normal import
It keeps the probe compilable even when the type has been removed
It turns Applet into an automatic module
completionChecks.explanation: A direct import would fail to compile on Java 26. A string-based reflection probe can report that the old type is gone.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the catch block print when java.applet.Applet is absent?
completionChecks.explanation: Class.forName throws ClassNotFoundException when the removed type cannot be found, so the catch block prints removed.
